title: Upon Ursley.
content:
Ursley, she thinks those velvet patches grace
    The candid temples of her comely face;
    But he will say, whoe'er those circlets seeth,
    They be but signs of Ursley's hollow teeth.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Ursley."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
